**Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC)**

**Exploratory Data Analysis**

**HackBio AI for Genomics Internship**

**Team valine-asparagine-alanine**

Uncovering drug response patterns, cancer-type sensitivity profiles, and genomic biomarkers of pharmacological outcome

**Dataset:** [GDSC Public Repository](https://github.com/HackBio-Internship/public_datasets/raw/refs/heads/main/GDSC.xlsx)

**Objective**

This notebook performs an exploratory analysis of the GDSC dataset to identify:

*   Drug sensitivity patterns
*   Cancer-specific therapeutic responses
*   Highly selective anti-cancer drugs
*   Genomic determinants of drug response
*   Precision oncology insights

**Scientific Background**

The Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC) project links:

*   Cancer cell lines
*   Genomic features
*   Drug response measurements

The aim is to understand how tumour molecular profiles influence therapeutic sensitivity.

**Dataset Overview:**

The GDSC dataset contains **162,103 drug-cell line sensitivity measurements** across **246 drugs**, **737 cancer cell lines**, and **30 TCGA cancer types**. Each row represents a single drug tested on a single cell line, with the following key variables: The Key Variables in the Datasets include _LN\_IC50, AUC, Z\_SCORE_ (sensitivity metrics); _DRUG\_NAME, TARGET, TARGET\_PATHWAY_ (therapeutic agents); _CELL\_LINE\_NAME, TCGA\_DESC, Microsatellite instability Status_ (Cancer Models); and CNA, Gene Expression, Methylation (Omics).

Each column represent:

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| COSMIC_ID | Unique Identifier on COSMIC |
| CELL_LINE_NAME | Name of cell line |
| TCGA_DESC | TCGA Code for cancer type |
| DRUG_ID | Drug ID |
| DRUG_NAME | Drug Name |
| LN_IC50 | Natural logarithm of the IC50 |
| AUC | Area under the dose dependent curve |
| Z_SCORE | Z score of deviations from the mean of zero; the closer to zero, the better |
| GDSC Tissue descriptor 1 | System description |
| GDSC Tissue descriptor 2 | Specific organ or tissue |
| Cancer Type (matching TCGA label) | Same as the TCGA |
| Microsatellite instability Status (MSI) | MSI-L (Minor instability), MSI-H; High instability, MSS: No instability or repaired mutation |
| Screen Medium | Medium for screening |
| Growth Properties | Growth properties |
| CNA | Is copy number altered? |
| Gene Expression | Is gene expressed? |
| Methylation | Is gene methylated? |
| TARGET | genetic target of drug |
| TARGET_PATHWAY | pathway of genetic target |

**Data Quality & Cleaning**

There are no missing values. There are duplicates in the datasets.

AUC validity: All values fall within 0 and 1

LN\_IC50 range: −8.64 to +13.82 (median = 3.27); 2% of values fall outside the 1st–99th percentile range

Preprocessing steps: Columns renamed for clarity; IC50\_uM back-transformed from LN\_IC50; binary flags converted to Boolean

**Drug Sensitivity Patterns**

**![](data:image/png;base64,)**

**Fig1: Distribution of Drug sensitivity**

**Top 5 Most Effective Drugs (Lowest LN\_IC50):**

**DRUG\_NAME Median\_LN\_IC50 Median\_AUC**

185 Romidepsin -5.317812 0.804303

45 Bortezomib -4.906318 0.772874

200 Sepantronium bromide -4.210507 0.174748

70 Docetaxel -3.990660 0.802501

67 Daporinad -3.902214 0.650521

**Top 5 Least Effective Drugs (Highest LN\_IC50):**

**DRUG\_NAME Median\_LN\_IC50 Median\_AUC**

244 ascorbate (vitamin C) 10.449790 0.976723

148 N-acetyl cysteine 9.944135 0.966516

245 glutathione 9.145341 0.974385

243 alpha-lipoic acid 7.705239 0.975969

214 Temozolomide 6.507988 0.979478

**Top 5 Most Variable Drugs (Potential Personalized Medicine Candidates):**

**DRUG\_NAME Var\_LN\_IC50**

105 Gemcitabine 8.655067

20 AZD5991 7.614386

67 Daporinad 7.483414

70 Docetaxel 6.486754

36 BI-2536 6.048389

The top five most effective drugs are: Romidepsin, Bortezomib, Sepantronium bromide, Docetaxel, and Daporinad.

The five least effective drugs are: Ascorbate (Vitamin C), N-acetyl cysteine, Glutathione, Alpha-lipoic acid, and Temozolomide.

Four out of five of these compounds _(ascorbate, N-acetyl cysteine, glutathione, and alpha-lipoic acid)_ are antioxidants or dietary supplements. Because they lack localized cytotoxic machinery, they do not induce cell death on their own.

The five most variable drugs are Gemcitabine, AZD5991, Daporinad, Docetaxel, and BI-2536.

The high response variance observed in drugs like **_Gemcitabine_** and **_AZD5991_** serves as the perfect statistical springboard for genomic biomarker tracking.

**Cancer Cell Line Analysis**

**Overall Sensitivity by Cancer Type**

| TCGA_DESC Median_LN_IC50 n_Cell_Lines Sample_Quality |
| --- |
| CLL 1.553044 2 ⚠ Very small sampleLAML 2.043654 24 RobustALL 2.063221 26 RobustDLBC 2.096066 33 RobustLCML 2.162442 10 ModerateMM 2.470976 16 ModerateACC 2.819815 1 ⚠ Very small sampleNB 2.851345 32 RobustMB 2.939177 4 ⚠ Very small sampleHNSC 3.119146 36 Robust |

LAML (median LN\_IC50 = 2.04), ALL (median LN\_IC50 = 2.06) and DLBC (median LN\_IC50 = 2.096) are the most drug-sensitive cancer types. PAAD (median LN\_IC50 = 4.17), MESO (median LN\_IC50 = 3.86), UCEC (median LN\_IC50 = 3.84) and LIHC (median LN\_IC50 = 3.83) are the most drug-resistant

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig2: Most drug-sensitive cancer types across all drugs**

Cancer types like LAML, ALL, DLBC, LCML, and MM are the most drug-sensitive cancer types. They are blood cancers. They show lower LN\_IC50 values. They usually respond better to many targeted agents, have faster proliferation and have greater dependence on oncogenic pathways. So seeing them among the most sensitive is biologically possible.

Overall drug sensitivity by cancer type was assessed using median LN\_IC50 values across all tested compounds. Hematologic malignancies, including LAML, ALL, DLBC, and LCML, showed the lowest median LN\_IC50 values, indicating increased drug sensitivity. In contrast, PAAD, UCEC, and LIHC exhibited the highest median LN\_IC50 values, consistent with clinically resistant tumour types. Cancer types with very small sample sizes (e.g., CLL and ACC) were interpreted cautiously.

**Cancer-Type-Selective Drug Responses**

| DRUG_NAME | Min_LN_IC50_x | Max_LN_IC50_x | Range | Most_Sensitive_Cancer | Min_LN_IC50_y | Least_Sensitive_Cancer | Max_LN_IC50_y | TARGET_PATHWAY_x | TARGET_PATHWAY_y | TARGET_PATHWAY |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Dasatinib | -7.024216 | 3.477284 | 10.501501 | LCML | -7.024216 | SKCM | 3.477284 | Other, kinases | Other, kinases | Other, kinases |
| Nilotinib | -4.954357 | 4.167787 | 9.122144 | LCML | -4.954357 | MESO | 4.167787 | ABL signaling | ABL signaling | ABL signaling |
| Gemcitabine | -5.156275 | 3.206833 | 8.363108 | CLL | -5.156275 | UCEC | 3.206833 | DNA replication | DNA replication | DNA replication |
| AZD5991 | -1.796554 | 6.223226 | 8.019780 | MM | -1.796554 | PAAD | 6.223226 | Apoptosis regulation | Apoptosis regulation | Apoptosis regulation |
| Bosutinib | -3.942034 | 3.904068 | 7.846102 | LCML | -3.942034 | GBM | 3.904068 | Other, kinases | Other, kinases | Other, kinases |
| Tozasertib | -2.161219 | 5.493971 | 7.655190 | DLBC | -2.161219 | PAAD | 5.493971 | Mitosis | Mitosis | Mitosis |

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig3: Top Cancer type selective drugs**

Dasatinib, Nilotinib and Bosutinib drugs selectively achieve better responses in LCML cancer by targeting the kinases and ABL signalling pathways, respectively, but they don't work better in SKCM, MESO and GBM cancers. Likewise, AZD5991 selectively works for MM cancers by targeting the DNA replication pathways. Tozasertib selectively work for DLBC by targeting the mitosis pathway.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig4: Cancer type sensitivity distribution**

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig5: Drug-Cancer type sensitivity heatmap**

**Genomic & Molecular Influences on Drug Response**

**LN\_IC50 vs AUC Correlation**

Pearson correlation r = 0.756 indicates a strong positive relationship. Spearman correlation ρ = 0.776 also confirms strong monotonic agreement. LN\_IC50 and AUC showed strong positive correlation (Pearson r = 0.756; Spearman ρ = 0.776; n = 5,000 sampled observations), indicating that both metrics capture related aspects of drug sensitivity. The correlation was not perfect, likely because AUC summarises the entire dose–response curve, whereas IC50 reflects only the midpoint response concentration.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig6: LN\_IC50vs AUC**

**MSI Status**

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig7: Drug sensitivity by MSI Status**

Mann–Whitney was used as LN\_IC50 distributions may not be normal, and it is robust to skewness/outliers. The p-value = 3.8078404168508164e-32

which is <0.001, so MSI-H tumours are significantly different. MSI-H tumours exhibited significantly greater drug sensitivity (lower LN\_IC50 median) than MSS/MSI-L tumours (median LN\_IC50 = 2.997 vs 3.293; Mann–Whitney U p < 0.001).

**MSI-H-Selective Drugs**

| DRUG_NAME | Delta_LN_IC50 (MSI-H - MSS median) | p_value | bonferroni_p | TARGET_PATHWAY |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Methotrexate | -2.427830 | 2.323654e-05 | 0.005716 | DNA replication |
| VE821 | -1.368650 | 1.428942e-04 | 0.035152 | Genome integrity |
| GSK2110183B | -1.248714 | 1.319419e-04 | 0.032458 | PI3K/MTOR signaling |
| 5-Fluorouracil | -1.096814 | 2.829482e-05 | 0.006961 | Other |
| AZD6738 | -1.075234 | 2.125003e-05 | 0.005228 | Genome integrity |
| Oxaliplatin | -0.904644 | 3.365266e-06 | 0.000828 | DNA replication |
| Ipatasertib | -0.870852 | 3.004201e-05 | 0.007390 | PI3K/MTOR signaling |
| Uprosertib | -0.855349 | 1.740440e-07 | 0.000043 | PI3K/MTOR signaling |
| Afuresertib | -0.598986 | 8.640339e-05 | 0.021255 | PI3K/MTOR signaling |

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig8: MSI-H Selective drugs**

After Bonferroni correction across 233 drugs, 10 compounds showed significant MSI-H selectivity, particularly agents targeting DNA replication and genome integrity pathways. This is biologically consistent with the defective mismatch repair and elevated replication stress characteristic of MSI-H tumours.

**Growth Properties**

Suspension-growing cell lines demonstrated significantly greater drug sensitivity than adherent cell lines (median LN\_IC50 = 2.44 vs 3.52; Mann–Whitney U p < 0.001).

**Drugs Preferentially Effective in Suspension Cells**

| DRUG_NAME | Delta_LN_IC50 | p_value | bonferroni_p | TARGET_PATHWAY |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AZD5991 | -4.037533 | 6.857126e-28 | 1.686853e-25 | Apoptosis regulation |
| BI-2536 | -3.305943 | 3.062716e-38 | 7.534281e-36 | Cell cycle |
| ABT737 | -3.120846 | 5.073589e-37 | 1.248103e-34 | Apoptosis regulation |
| Alisertib | -3.106905 | 6.535422e-22 | 1.607714e-19 | Mitosis |
| Cytarabine | -3.064477 | 1.429757e-23 | 3.517201e-21 | Other |
| Navitoclax | -3.029453 | 9.917882e-34 | 2.439799e-31 | Apoptosis regulation |
| Methotrexate | -2.906178 | 6.661953e-11 | 1.638841e-08 | DNA replication |
| Gemcitabine | -2.848927 | 3.327317e-20 | 8.185199e-18 | DNA replication |
| JQ1 | -2.694382 | 5.051174e-33 | 1.242589e-30 | Chromatin other |
| Tozasertib | -2.586193 | 1.679244e-11 | 4.130941e-09 | Mitosis |

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Fig9: Suspension-selective drugs**

Drugs preferentially active in suspension cells included AZD5991, BI-2536, ABT737, Alisertib, Navitoclax, Cytarabine, and Methotrexate, all of which have established relevance in hematologic malignancies. These drugs mainly target DNA replication, apoptosis, the cell cycle and mitosis. However, this effect is likely confounded by cancer type composition, as suspension-growing lines predominantly represent hematologic cancers, whereas adherent lines are mainly derived from solid tumours.

**Genomic, transcriptomic and epigenomic influence on drug response**

**CNA:**

CNA available median: 3.269899

No CNA median: 3.508225

P-value: 0.007392764007602847

Delta median: -0.23832599999999982

**Gene Expression:**

expr\_yes median: 3.283111

expr\_no median: 2.830496

P-value: 2.912858913302848e-30

Delta: 0.45261499999999977

**Methylation:**

methylation\_yes median: 3.272621

methylation\_no median: 3.211147

P-value: 0.3424236592391552

Delta: 0.06147400000000003

Availability of CNA and gene expression data was significantly associated with LN\_IC50 differences (Mann–Whitney U p < 0.01), although effect sizes were small (delta median < 0.5 LN\_IC50 units). These associations likely reflect cancer type composition bias rather than direct causal effects of genomic data availability on drug response. However, Methylation does not have any effect on drug response (p=0.342).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

  
**Fig10: Mean Z-Score heatmap (cancer type × target pathway)**

Correlation analysis demonstrated strong concordance between LN\_IC50 and AUC measurements, while pathway-level heatmaps highlighted lineage-specific drug sensitivity patterns across cancer types.

**Findings**

*   Hematologic malignancies demonstrated the greatest overall drug sensitivity. Cancer types such as LAML, ALL, DLBC, and MM exhibited the lowest median LN\_IC50 values, aligning with their known clinical responsiveness to chemotherapy and targeted therapies.
*   Pancreatic adenocarcinoma (PAAD) emerged as the most drug-resistant cancer type, with the highest median LN\_IC50 value (4.17). This observation is consistent with the aggressive nature of pancreatic cancer and its limited therapeutic responsiveness in clinical settings.
*   MEK inhibitors displayed strong melanoma-specific activity. Trametinib and PD0325901 were particularly selective for SKCM cell lines, reflecting the frequent activation of the MAPK pathway through BRAF and RAS mutations in melanoma.
*   MSI-H tumors showed increased drug sensitivity compared with MSS/MSI-L tumors, especially toward DNA-damaging agents and ATR inhibitors. These findings support the biological vulnerability of mismatch repair–deficient tumors to replication stress and DNA damage response targeting.
*   Drugs targeting mitosis and DNA replication pathways were among the most broadly potent compounds across cancer types, highlighting the dependence of rapidly proliferating cancer cells on these core cellular processes.
*   Antioxidant compounds such as Vitamin C, N-acetylcysteine (NAC), and glutathione exhibited the highest IC50 values, indicating minimal cytotoxic activity. Their inclusion illustrates the broad pharmacologic scope of the GDSC screening platform.

**Limitations**

*   The CNA, Gene Expression, and Methylation variables represent only the availability of molecular profiling data rather than actual quantitative genomic measurements. Access to continuous genomic features would allow more detailed mechanistic and biomarker analyses.
*   Certain cancer types had very limited sample sizes, including CLL (n=2), ACC (n=1), and MB (n=4). Results for these groups should therefore be interpreted cautiously.
*   Observed associations involving growth properties or genomic data availability are likely influenced by confounding due to cancer type composition rather than direct biological effects.
*   All analyses were performed using in vitro cancer cell line models. Drug responses observed in cell lines may not fully translate to patient tumors or clinical outcomes.
*   Some IC50 estimates may be less reliable because they were extrapolated beyond the experimentally tested drug concentration ranges.
